name: Signal Rail
description: A text-first governance system for AI-assisted project work.
runtime: AI-assisted project work with plain-text files
purpose: Keep personal and project material readable while work grows, branches, changes shape, and resumes after interruption.
operating_question: What kind of material is this, and where should it live?
operating_model: Signal Rail is mainly designed to be operated with an AI agent during real work.
The agent reads the entry layer, activates the lateral kernel, closes the working frame, and helps route material into the right document without mixing levels.
The human stays in charge of meaning, authority, and approval.
quick_start: Create a Signal Rail instance in the target project area.
Use init_signal_rail.bat for Windows bootstrap, or manually copy the required files while preserving file names, marker structure, canonical roles, and entry flow.
Ask the AI agent to read 00_runtime_entry.txt, then 06_ai_to_ai.txt.
Close host project, working object, active mode, source scope, and authority before substantive work.

rails.role: valid entry, minimum read, and reading boundaries
project identity, perimeter, and reading frame
identity constants that should be hard to reopen
current live state, blocker, active work, and next move
choices already taken, already in effect, and already won against alternatives
important unresolved material that still needs motion or placement
agent operating behavior inside a Signal Rail instance
guided paths for safer starts, rebuilds, reviews, and routing passes
real technical topology, entrypoints, sensitive surfaces, and minimal runbook
re-entry support and continuity, not canonical project truth
lateral captures during an active pass before routing or discard
useful material that is not active now
closed, historical, duplicate, or no-longer-live material

boundary_model.meaning: this repository, the reusable Signal Rail source
a copy of Signal Rail used inside or beside a real project
optional local interface for reading, staging, previewing, and writing a live instance

workstation.purpose: Local workstation for reading canonicals, checking runtime state, staging writes, previewing output, and writing back to matching .txt files when live folder access is available.
workstation.authority: Does not replace the canonicals and does not change authority.

prevents: technical reality buried inside generic notes
continuity pretending to be project truth
a strong sentence becoming a decision
a live blocker becoming project identity
a hypothesis entering current work too early
a handoff note becoming canonical truth
a deployed instance being mistaken for the host project
